definition: China's commodity demand is structurally decelerating across all major commodities except copper. Bai-Perron structural breaks identified for steel (2013, t-stat 4.2) and aluminum (2018, t-stat 2.8), but no significant break found for copper. China's incremental steel demand share fell from 90%+ (2005-2015) to 35-45% (2020-2025), while copper remains at 55-65% of incremental demand growing 4-6% p.a.
evidence: Bai-Perron structural break: steel at 2013 (t-stat 4.2), aluminum at 2018 (t-stat 2.8)
No significant structural break identified for copper demand
China incremental steel demand share: 90%+ (2005-2015) to 35-45% (2020-2025)
Copper at 55-65% of incremental demand, growing 4-6% p.a.
Driven by grid investment (+15-20% YoY), EV production (~10M units 2025), renewables (300+ GW/yr)
Total commodity intensity per GDP declining 2-3%/yr but does not negate copper exception
open_questions: Can India replace China's demand growth at meaningful scale?
At what point does China's copper demand intensity per unit GDP begin to decline?
How much double-counting exists between China demand and energy transition demand estimates?
